config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan hak dasar itulah yang mencegah penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Saya memahami bahwa dalam keadaan tertentu kepentingan umum bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat ketat, jelas, dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Jika prioritas selalu diberikan pada masyarakat tanpa batas, individu yang lemah akan paling mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once you make that the default, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to trample unpopular people. From a United States perspective, I see individual rights as the foundation that keeps society legitimate in the first place, whether we are talking about free speech, due process, or privacy. That said, I do think society can limit individual freedom in narrow cases like public health or safety, but only when the limit is clearly justified and not a blank check for power. If the tradeoff is framed as society versus the individual, I side with protecting the individual first, because a society that ignores rights is not really worth defending.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai terlalu longgar dan akhirnya jadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Benar bahwa ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau ketertiban umum yang bisa membatasi hak individu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus proporsional, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas ingin begitu. Kalau hak individu selalu dianggap nomor dua, yang paling rentan justru minoritas, orang miskin, dan mereka yang suaranya paling lemah. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengurus kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become a cover for abuse, censorship, and discrimination. I do not buy the idea that society is automatically healthier if it gets to override individual rights first; a society is only legitimate when it protects unpopular people, minorities, and dissenters from the majority. That said, I do think there are limited cases where public health or immediate safety can justify temporary restrictions, but those need strict limits, clear evidence, and real accountability. If the balance always tilts toward society, then the rights are not rights anymore—they are permissions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, frasa “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan pada minoritas, orang lemah, atau mereka yang tidak punya akses kuasa, jadi saya tidak percaya itu aman dijadikan default. Pembatasan hak memang bisa dibenarkan untuk kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau ketertiban umum, tetapi harus sempit, terukur, dan bisa diawasi ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang sanggup melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of framing has too often been used to excuse overreach by governments and majorities. I do not see rights as a luxury that society can override whenever it is convenient; free speech, due process, privacy, and equal protection are what keep society legitimate in the first place. At the same time, I am not saying individual rights are absolute in every case, since public health and immediate safety can justify narrow limits. But those limits need to be temporary, evidence-based, and tightly controlled, or else “society’s interests” becomes a blank check to silence and control people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
